Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

(defect) pack_generic.h should be called lustre_swab.h.

(defect) When adding new includes please use the following order:

linux headers
libcfs headers
lnet headers
lustre/include/lustre/* headers
lustre/include/*
*_internal.h

Within each section try to keep things in alphabetical order instead of adding the new includes at the end. This will help to avoid merge conflicts and multiple inclusions of the same header.

Patch Set 2:

Also why are we adding llog_swab.h and pack_generic.h instead of just using one header?

Patch Set 2:

New header files reflect the names of the .c files, along with their contents.  If you'd like all swab functions to be in one .h and one .c file, (lustre_swab.h) I'm happy to accommodate.

Patch Set 5:

If you want tickets up the code style in the renamed file, the code style fixes should be done in a separate patch _before_ the file is renamed. Otherwise, hit will have difficulty detecting a file that is both renamed and has lots of modifications, and we lose all of the file history.

Patch Set 8: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Sorry, half of the functions defined in llog_swab.h just have nothing to do with llog. Let's just have 1 header for swabbing functions. I don't care how many .c files there are.

We don't need to rearrange all of the includes. Just add new included in the right place. This will minimize the chance of a merge conflict.

Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

OK fine.

Patch Set 8: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

Looks mostly good. Need to move these new headers out of the "userspace install" directory and in with the regular internal headers.

parents.subject: LU-7382 llite: Fix iovec references accounting in ll_file_aio_read/write

commit.subject: LU-6401 headers: move swab functions to new header files
commit.message:
LU-6401 headers: move swab functions to new header files

Create headers for pack_generic.c and llog_swab.c
Reference only where needed.

Signed-off-by: Ben Evans <bevans@cray.com>
Change-Id: I0942d2b7e3d60994c43832a94625fa300bac6617
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/16339
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
